behaviors.description: Reject mismatched HMAC signature with 401 and do not forward body.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (HTTP raw body/header extraction -> signature verifier -> queue call), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS/subprocess is required; in-process app wiring is enough. MOCK-HIDE: mocking verifier or request shaping can hide raw-body mismatch bugs. Rejection gate: System was considered, but behavior text only requires 'rejected with 401' and 'not forwarded' and does not require real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real webhook handler stack in-process (real request parsing/raw-body capture path) with a stub queue. Send a request whose header digest is computed for different bytes than the sent body. Assert HTTP 401 and zero enqueue calls.

behaviors.description: Persist to queue before returning HTTP 200 acknowledgment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is explicit cross-module ordering (handler vs persistent queue write). REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local in-process modules and local persistent backend; no real external network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: a mocked queue that resolves immediately can hide incorrect ack-before-persist ordering. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text is ordering, not real network/OS fault behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real queue adapter (backed by local test persistence) and instrument response completion timestamp. Force queue write latency and assert write-commit event occurs before HTTP 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream event processing completion.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is asynchronous handoff between enqueue/ack and downstream processor lifecycle. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network needed; controlled in-process slow downstream task is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: a fully mocked downstream path may not expose accidental await/blocking in handler. Rejection gate: System rejected because text requires non-blocking behavior, not real infrastructure faults.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + real queue + downstream worker in-process. Make downstream processing intentionally long-running. Assert response is sent promptly after enqueue and before processing completion signal.

behaviors.description: Response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock under concurrent slow downstream load.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit wall-clock SLA under concurrency is sensitive to real HTTP server/event-loop scheduling and contention. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can miss timing regressions and queue contention effects seen only under real request concurrency. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly requires 'within 3 seconds wall-clock time' under concurrent load, a real infrastructure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service as a running process, create concurrent HTTP traffic while earlier events are processed slowly, and measure end-to-end latency for new webhook requests. Assert p95 (or strict per-request check for this case) remains under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: Duplicate event ID is not written to queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedup correctness is at handler->persistent queue/idempotency-store boundary. REAL-INFRA: local wired modules with real persistence adapter are enough; no external OS/network requirement. MOCK-HIDE: mocked repository may not enforce uniqueness/idempotency behavior. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text is duplicate-write prevention, not real network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Send two webhook requests with the same event ID through the real handler and real persistence-backed queue in a test environment. Assert first is accepted, second is not written again (single stored/enqueued record).

behaviors.description: When queue is full, return 503 and include Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure mapping is across queue-capacity signal -> HTTP translation in handler. REAL-INFRA: can be proven with in-process app and real queue module configured to small capacity; no real network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: mocked queue-full errors can miss real adapter error shapes/codes. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text concerns status/header mapping, not OS/network-level failures.
behaviors.test_description: Configure real queue capacity to a small limit, fill it, then send another webhook request through the handler. Assert HTTP 503 and presence/format of Retry-After header.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow events.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this validates queue module + persistence interaction (configured limit enforcement), beyond single-function logic. REAL-INFRA: local persistence (DB/filesystem) in-process test is sufficient; no external network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: in-memory/mock queue may not reproduce persistence-backed limit behavior. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text does not require real external infrastructure beyond local module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with a known capacity, enqueue up to the limit, then enqueue one more. Assert overflow operation is rejected and no extra record is persisted.
